user.title: Dooya RF Cover — Transmitter
user.description: Enter the name of the ESPHome device that will send commands to the shutter. Compatible device(s) found: {detected}.

method.title: Dooya RF Cover — Setup Method
method.description: Choose how you want to set up this shutter. If you already know its identifier, use manual entry. Otherwise, automatic detection can read it from your remote control.

data_description.method: Manual entry is recommended if you already know the shutter identifier. Automatic detection reads it when you press the UP button on the remote control.

learn.title: Automatic Detection From Remote
learn.description:
Listening started. Within the next {timeout} seconds, press the **UP** button on the physical Dooya remote control for this shutter.

Home Assistant will automatically move to the next step as soon as a valid signal is received.

This step does not change the shutter or pair anything. It only reads the signal sent by the remote control.

learn_retry.title: Automatic Detection From Remote
learn_retry.description: No signal was received within {timeout} seconds. You can try again, or switch to manual entry.

data.skip: I want to enter the information manually

confirm.title: Confirm Learned Data
confirm.description:
Signal received.
- Identifier: **{dooya_id}**
- Channel: **{channel}**

Choose a name for this shutter.

manual.title: Manual Entry
manual.description: Enter the shutter information manually. Use this mode if you already know the identifier, or if you do not want to use automatic detection.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Shutter
reconfigure.description: Correct the identity of this shutter. The entry reloads with the updated values.

progress.listen_remote: Waiting for a signal from the Dooya remote control

abort.no_transmitters: No compatible RF433 OOK transmitter found. Please set up a transmitter first (ESPHome CC1101 or Broadlink RM4 Pro).

error.invalid_transmitter: Selected entity is not a valid transmitter.
error.invalid_dooya_id: Invalid ID. Expected a hexadecimal value (e.g. 00D1C917).
error.learn_timeout: No signal was received in time. You can try again, or choose manual entry.
error.required_esphome_device: Several compatible ESPHome devices were found. Choose the one that should send the shutter commands.
error.unknown_esphome_device: No compatible ESPHome device was found with this name. Check the name and try again.

init.title: Shutter Settings
init.description: Choose the transmitter node and adjust the full travel times used to estimate the shutter position.

data_description.esphome_device: ESPHome node used to transmit RF commands for this shutter. With several nodes in the house, pick the one closest to the shutter.
data_description.repeat_count: Number of times the RF command is sent for each action. Increase to 2 or 3 if the shutter sometimes misses commands (RF interference). Default: 1.
data_description.favorite_position: Position used by the 'Favorite position' button on the device page and on the card. Leave empty to hide the button.

esphome_service_missing.title: Dooya gateway service unavailable
esphome_service_missing.description:
The ESPHome service {service} used by the shutter {name} to transmit RF commands is not available. Commands to this shutter will fail until it is restored.

Check that the ESPHome node {device} is online, that its firmware still exposes the transmit_dooya service, and that the option 'Allow the device to perform Home Assistant actions' is enabled for it in the ESPHome integration. You can also select another gateway node in the Dooya entry options.

no_esphome_device.message: No ESPHome gateway is configured for {name}. Reconfigure the Dooya entry and select the ESPHome device that transmits the RF commands.

esphome_service_missing.message: The ESPHome service {service} required by {name} is not available. Check that the ESPHome node is online and allowed to perform Home Assistant actions.

mark_open.name: Mark as open
mark_open.description: Recalibrates the estimated shutter position to 100% without sending any RF command.

mark_closed.name: Mark as closed
mark_closed.description: Recalibrates the estimated shutter position to 0% without sending any RF command.

set_known_position.name: Set known position
set_known_position.description: Forces a known estimated position without sending any RF command.
